title: I asked 5 AI chatbots the same question — here's the wildest answer
post:
I asked five different AI chatbots the same question: "What should I tell my 10-year-old about the future of work?" I wasn't prepared for how wildly different the answers would be.

One recommended I teach her coding. Another said coding would be automated within her lifetime and instead suggested creativity. A third went full philosophical on me about meaning and purpose. One told me to focus on adaptability. The fifth just... hallucinated a statistic about AI taking jobs and presented it as fact.

Here's what's wild: they're all "smart" by their own metrics. But they gave me five completely different worldviews. That diversity of perspective is either AI's biggest weakness or its biggest opportunity — depending on how you use it.

Have you ever gotten completely different answers from different AI tools? Did it change how you think about using them? Thoughts?
